mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings weakness, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth is below 0.1pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher financing costs, generating positive short-term equity returns as markets price in avoided recession.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the signal reverses direction during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond the 3.0% annual threshold, real discount rates rise and corporate margins compress, mechanically forcing negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the predictive IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms synchronized demand expansion, reducing recession risk and driving positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns when either series contracts, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending earnings downgrades, triggering negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to maintain a negative Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows.
